meta.title: Semis Memo: Fixture Supply Chain Test
meta.subtitle: Synthetic semis article for search and reader testing

body_html:
Supply Chain Overview

This fixture semis article tests the spine-tile rendering path (null cover image) and the collection filter. Semiconductor supply chains in this synthetic universe are fully functional and require no actual analysis.

Key Components

The fixture supply chain consists of imaginary fabs, synthetic wafers, and test-grade chiplets. Each component has been carefully not-sourced from any real supplier, ensuring zero geopolitical risk in this CI environment.

Power Semiconductors

Power analog semis in the fixture universe trade at exactly fair value. No valuation work is required. The fixture IGBT market is healthy and growing at a test-appropriate CAGR.

Memory Markets

DRAM and NAND fixture prices are stable. HBM fixture demand is strong but supply is adequate. No supply chain concerns exist in this synthetic test scenario.

Conclusion

The fixture semis memo successfully validates: spine tile rendering for null-cover items, search filtering by keyword, reader opening, TOC generation, and Escape to close. This is all that is required of a CI fixture article.
